steps.title: Set up the scale ratio
steps.content: The scale gives $1$ inch (model) $= \frac{17}{3}$ feet (actual). Equivalently, $\frac{17}{3}$ feet of actual length corresponds to $1$ inch in the model.

steps.title: Find inches per foot
steps.content: Divide both sides by $\frac{17}{3}$: $1$ foot of actual length corresponds to $\frac{1}{17/3} = \frac{3}{17}$ inch in the model.

steps.title: Scale up to x feet
steps.content:
For $x$ feet of actual length, multiply:
$$x \cdot \frac{3}{17} = \frac{3x}{17} \text{ inches}$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• $\frac{3x}{(17)(12)}$: includes an unnecessary factor of $12$ — eliminate.
  • $\frac{17x}{(12)(3)}$: wrong ratio and extra $12$ — eliminate.
  • $\frac{3x}{17}$: matches the derived expression.
  • $\frac{17x}{3}$: this converts inches to feet, not the reverse — eliminate.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
